architecture.summary: TRUSTMARGIN is a training-free arbitration layer that selects between two frozen-model answers: one from direct generation (parametric memory) and one from RAG-augmented generation (retrieved evidence). It scores both candidates using the model's own token likelihoods, combining a parametric-prior margin (testing whether the model's memory accepts the retrieved answer) and an evidence-binding margin (measuring question-specific support from passages while discounting passage-only salience). The system operates post-generation on existing candidate answers without fine-tuning, external judges, or additional decoding.

biblio.title: TrustMargin: Training-Free Arbitration between Parametric Memory and Retrieved Evidence in Large Language Models

claims.statement: TRUSTMARGIN consistently improves over Direct generation and BM25-RAG across 2WIKIMQA and CWQA with three LLaMA scales
TRUSTMARGIN recovers part of the Direct/RAG oracle gap
TRUSTMARGIN generalizes to multiple training-free RAG pipelines
TRUSTMARGIN is training-free, plug-and-play, does not require fine-tuning, external judges, or additional generation
The parametric-prior margin tests whether the model's parametric memory accepts the retrieved answer
The evidence-binding margin discounts passage-only salience and measures question-specific support from retrieved passages
